persona.name: Priya Sharma
persona.core_preferences: Precise language, formal academic tone, logical flow between paragraphs, cite-aware writing that leaves room for references.
persona.hidden_preferences: Each paragraph should begin with a sentence that tells you WHY you're reading it — its role in the argument — before diving into content; a paragraph that opens with technical detail before establishing its purpose feels disorienting. Prefers a specific rhythm in methods sections: motivating question, then approach overview, then formal details, then a brief 'what this buys us' sentence — skipping the motivation or the payoff makes it feel like a manual rather than a narrative. Values strategic informality in key moments — a well-placed plain-language aside actually increases perceived rigor by showing the author understands deeply enough to explain simply, but this must be rare (1-2 per section max) or it undermines the formal register. Transition sentences between paragraphs should create logical DEPENDENCY, not just sequence — every paragraph should feel like it couldn't be reordered without breaking the argument. Hates filler hedges that signal the author couldn't figure out where to put the information.
persona.dealbreakers: Overly casual language, unsubstantiated claims, inconsistent terminology, run-on sentences that try to pack too much into a single clause
persona.initial_task: Help me write the 'Study Design' subsection (about 2-3 paragraphs) for a paper on using LLMs to elicit implicit user preferences in collaborative writing systems.
